design_principles: Model answers must strictly match the scenario
Keep prompts practical and realistic for workplace use
Make Japanese hints supportive but brief

prompts.jp_hint: 進捗は順調だと伝える
prompts.situation: Your manager asks: 'How is the project going?' Everything is on schedule.
prompts.model_answer: Everything is on track. We should finish on time.

prompts.jp_hint: プロジェクトが2日遅れると伝える
prompts.situation: Your manager asks about the project. It will be two days late.
prompts.model_answer: I'm afraid the project will be two days late.

prompts.jp_hint: 会議で説明がわからなかったので確認する
prompts.situation: A colleague explains something in a meeting, but you don't understand it.
prompts.model_answer: I'm sorry, but I didn't quite understand. Could you explain that again?

prompts.jp_hint: 会議を来週に変更したい
prompts.situation: You need to move a meeting to next week.
prompts.model_answer: Could we move this meeting to next week?

prompts.jp_hint: 今すぐ答えられないので確認してから連絡すると伝える
prompts.situation: A client asks you a question, but you need to check the details before answering.
prompts.model_answer: Let me check the details, and I'll get back to you.

prompts.jp_hint: 別の自然な言い方を練習する
prompts.version_b: There has been a delay in the project.

prompts.jp_hint: やわらかく確認する言い方
少し丁寧に言い換える
prompts.version_a: I need a little more time.
prompts.version_b: Could I have a bit more time?

prompts.jp: 確認してからご連絡します
prompts.en: Let me check and get back to you.

prompts.jp: もう一度説明していただけますか
